places.name: Kurukshetra — The Prophecy
places.modernInfo: The Bhavishya Purana prophecies about Kurukshetra extend to modern times — it predicted the coming of Islam, Christianity and even British rule in India.

scenes.title: The future is written at Kurukshetra
scenes.description: The Bhavishya Purana (Purana of the Future) begins at Kurukshetra. It contains prophecies about future avatars, future kings, the deterioration of Kali Yuga, and the eventual arrival of Kalki — Vishnu's final avatar who will end this age.

places.name: Konark — Sun Temple
places.modernInfo: UNESCO World Heritage Site. The temple is built as a giant chariot of the Sun god with 24 wheels (representing hours) and 7 horses (days of the week).

scenes.title: The chariot of time
scenes.description: The Bhavishya Purana, dedicated to the Sun god Surya, describes Konark as the centre of solar worship. The temple's 24 wheels are sundials — you can tell time to the minute by the shadows they cast.
